bugs.bug_description: The DP stores the last element value of an increasing subsequence for each XOR, but it keeps the *largest* possible last value (by updating only when dp[new_x] < num) instead of the *smallest* one. The correct approach is to keep the minimal last value (dp[new_x] = min(dp[new_x], num) or update when dp[new_x] > num). Keeping a larger last value can block later extensions that require a smaller last value, leading to missing reachable XORs (false negatives). Additionally, initializing dp[0] to 0 rather than -1 (or -inf) may incorrectly prevent using 0 as the first element in some cases.
bugs.reasoning: Because the algorithm does not maintain the minimal possible last value for each XOR, it can fail to recognize some XOR values that are achievable via an increasing subsequence. This violates the problem requirements, so the code is buggy.
